question: When can I register for a trek?
answer:
Registration opens exactly one month (30 days) before the trek date and closes one week (7 days) before the excursion.

Example: For a trek on April 15th:
• Registration opens: March 16th
• Registration closes: April 8th
• Trek takes place: April 15th

Early registration is recommended as spots may fill up quickly!

question: Who can join a trek?
answer:
Any registered user can join a trek while registrations are open, provided they meet any specific requirements for that particular trek.

Requirements:
• Must have a valid account on our platform
• Must be at least 18 years old (or accompanied by an adult)
• Must accept our safety terms and conditions
• Some treks may have specific fitness requirements

Children under 14 must be accompanied by a responsible adult. Please check individual trek descriptions for age restrictions.

question: How does the 0–5 rating system work?
answer:
Our difficulty rating system is based on terrain, distance, elevation gain, and technical challenges.

0 → Unknown/Unrated: New route not yet evaluated
1 → Very Easy: Flat terrain, short distance (1-3km), suitable for everyone including children
2 → Easy: Gentle slopes, moderate distance (3-6km), minimal elevation gain
3 → Moderate: Some steep sections, 6-10km, requires basic fitness level
4 → Demanding: Steep inclines, 10-15km, significant elevation, requires good fitness
5 → Very Difficult: Technical terrain, 15+km, high elevation, for experienced hikers only

💡 Beginner tip: Start with 1-2 rated treks and work your way up!

question: Can I add images to my comment?
answer:
Yes! Users may upload images with their comments to share their experience visually.

Image Guidelines:
• Maximum file size: 5MB per image
• Supported formats: JPG, PNG
• Maximum 3 images per comment
• Images should be relevant to the trek experience
• No inappropriate or copyrighted content

Images are automatically resized for optimal display. They help future trekkers get a better sense of what to expect!

📸 Pro tip: Capture trail conditions, viewpoints, and interesting flora/fauna!

question: Can I filter treks by island?
answer:
Yes! Our comprehensive filtering system allows you to find treks based on multiple criteria.

• By Island
• By Difficulty (1-5 rating)
• By Municipality
• By Average Rating

You can combine multiple filters to find the perfect trek for your preferences and skill level.

question: Are the treks suitable for beginners?
answer:
Absolutely! We offer treks for all experience levels, from complete beginners to seasoned hikers.

For Beginners (Rating 1-2):
• Well-marked trails with minimal obstacles
• Short to medium distances (1-6km)
• Gentle elevation changes
• Frequent rest stops
• Experienced guides who provide basic hiking tips

What to Bring as a Beginner:
• Comfortable walking shoes or hiking boots
• At least 1 liter of water per person
• Sun protection (hat, sunscreen, sunglasses)
• Light snack or energy bars
• Weather-appropriate clothing (layers recommended)
• Small backpack to carry your essentials

Our guides are trained to adjust the pace to accommodate all participants. Don't hesitate to communicate any concerns!

👣 New to hiking? Join our 'First Trek' workshops held monthly!

question: What happens if the weather is bad?
answer:
Safety first! Treks may be rescheduled or cancelled due to inclement weather.

Weather Policy:
• Decisions are made 24 hours before the trek
• All registered participants receive email/SMS notifications
• Rescheduled treks maintain your registration automatically
• Full refunds available for cancelled treks if rescheduling isn't possible

☔ We monitor multiple weather sources including AEMET and local mountain forecasts.

question: Can I cancel my registration?
answer:
Yes, you can cancel your registration any time without any penalty applied.

But please act wisely; we plan our treks and assign guides depending on how many people will attend.
